title: Fuel Mixture Calibration
description: Dial in a safe oxidizer-to-fuel ratio for a hobbyist model rocket before launch.

dialogue.text: Nova here. We'll tune a conservative training mix and log every measurement before anything goes near a motor casing.
Work clean and grounded: anti-static mat down, safety glasses on, and only small-batch ingredients on the bench.

options.text: Bench is staged and PPE is on.
I still have clutter and no grounded mat.

dialogue.text: Hard stop. No mixing with static risk or loose hardware nearby. Clear the bench, ground the station, then re-open the prep checklist.

options.text: Bench is now grounded and clear. Restart prep.

dialogue.text: Record a baseline batch weight and verify parachute ballast mass before calibration so your flight profile stays predictable.

options.text: Baseline logged with scale + ballast mass.

dialogue.text: Choose your correction plan: fine-step binder tuning for precision, or a fresh reset batch if the first reading drifted badly.

options.text: Take the precision route with small binder steps.
Start a clean reset batch to eliminate drift.

dialogue.text: Add binder in tiny increments, remix, and re-weigh. If the ratio lands in your safe window, capture the reading in the log.

options.text: The ratio keeps drifting outside tolerance.

dialogue.text: Good call. Dump the suspect batch, tare again, and rebuild from the baseline recipe before another tolerance check.
If drift persists, check humidity, scale zero, and scoop contamination. Fix one variable at a time, then rerun measurement.
Final gate: confirm the calibrated mix supports a stable launch profile and generate a logged launch token before sign-off.

options.text: Validation launch logged. Calibration is stable.

dialogue.text: Great work. You produced a safer, repeatable mix plan with evidence instead of vibes. That's real rocketry discipline.

options.text: Logbook updated. Ready for the next test window.
